title: Don't bind /etc/host and /etc/resolv.conf if network is not present
body:
If there's no network present in the container, don't bind
the /etc/hosts file as any changes to from the caller will
be lost when run exits.  Ditto the /etc/resolv.conf file.

Addresses: #2478

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body: Looks like either we have a bug or a test needs to be adjusted.

network error in an unrelated test, bors be kind this time:
bors retry
I've repushed, the from.bats test was apparently still failing, but it works in my sandbox.  I'm not sure the push will fix anything, but we'll see.

Turns out the `configureNetwork` changes holds when doing `buildah run`, but does not hold when there's a `RUN` statement in a Containerfile.  I played with it a bit last night, and think I've a solution for both, more testing today.
All happy green test buttons.  This is ready to go in with appropriate reviews.  I would like a @nalind head nod if he's got a minute or two.

bors r+
@TomSweeneyRedHat could you look to see if we have the same issue with Podman?
Created an investigative issue on Podman so I won't forget.   https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7206
